name: Bahrain

outbound_official.note: No official Bahrain government outbound visa-list web page located. The Bahrain Ministry of Foreign Affairs (mofa.gov.bh) website blocks programmatic access (HTTP 405 on all endpoints). Their outbound travel guidance for Bahrain citizens is delivered exclusively through the Wejhaty mobile app (referenced at mofa.gov.bh/en/travel-safe-wejhaty and bahrain.bh Travelling Abroad page) rather than a publicly accessible structured web page. Rely on destination-side data.

visa_free.nationality: GCC nationals (Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates, Oman, Qatar)
visa_free.notes: Citizens of all GCC member states do not require a visa to enter Bahrain. GCC citizens enjoy right of residence and movement among GCC states. Movement by national ID card is permitted for GCC nationals in Bahrain (Saudi Arabia is making final arrangements for ID-based movement).

e_visa.notes: Eligible for Bahrain e-visa. Max stay subject to visa type selected. Terms and conditions apply.
Listed as a category on the official e-visa eligibility page. Likely refers to EU passport holders not individually enumerated.

visa_policy.notes: GCC nationals (Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, UAE, Oman, Qatar) are visa-free. Bahrain itself is a GCC member. 211 nationalities are eligible for online e-visa per evisa.gov.bh (official NPRA system). 204 nationalities are listed on the official on-arrival visa page (evisa.gov.bh/list-of-onarrival-visa-country.html) - all marked 'Conditions apply'. The exact conditions (stay duration, fee, eligibility specifics) per nationality require clicking through on the individual country pages. Per the NPRA e-visa service page, GCC residents (regardless of nationality) can also apply for a 2-week single entry or 3-month multiple entry e-visa.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on official Bahraini government sources. Bahraini citizenship (naturalization) requires 10 years consecutive legal residency for Arabs and 20 years for non-Arabs, property ownership, good command of Arabic, and good conduct - per npra.gov.bh. This is a standard naturalization pathway, not a CBI program. Source: https://www.npra.gov.bh/en/services/nationality/applying-for-bahraini-nationality

programs.name: Bahrain Golden Residency
programs.notes: Bahrain Golden Residency program offering permanent residency. Multiple eligibility tracks: (1) Property investors: minimum BHD 130,000 (USD 345,000) personal share value, may combine multiple properties; (2) Professionals: 5+ years continuous employment in Bahrain with average monthly basic salary exceeding BHD 2,000; (3) Resident retirees: 15+ years Bahrain employment, pension BHD 2,000+/month; (4) Non-resident retirees: pension BHD 4,000+/month; (5) Talented individuals: entrepreneurs, researchers, scientists, artists, innovators, athletes, other exceptional talent - subject to specialized committee review. Processing time: 5 working days (standard categories); up to 10 working days (talent category). Application fee: BHD 5; issuance fee BHD 300. No minimum stay requirement. Dependants (spouse, parents, children - no age limit) may be included. Golden Residency holders must obtain a separate work permit from LMRA to work. The real estate threshold was reduced from BHD 200,000 to BHD 130,000 in November 2025. Source: https://goldenresidency.gov.bh/eligibility and https://goldenresidency.gov.bh/faqs/

programs.name: Investor Residency (via NPRA / bahrain.bh)
programs.notes: The bahrain.bh national portal lists residency for foreign investors for periods of 2, 5, and 10 years, with investment share not less than BHD 100,000. This appears to be a separate investor residence permit distinct from the Golden Residency program. Additional details not fully available from official page text extracted.

programs.name: Golden Residency - Talented Individuals Track
programs.eligibility: Entrepreneurs, researchers/scientists, artists/creatives, innovators (technology/business), championship-winning athletes, and individuals with exceptional leadership or community service achievements. Subject to specialized committee review.
programs.notes: Part of the Bahrain Golden Residency program. No specific investment threshold for the talent category - approved based on merit and committee review.

conditional_access.conditions: Traveller must hold a valid GCC country (Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, or UAE) residence permit with at least 3 months remaining validity. Entitles holder to apply for a 3-month multiple-entry visit eVisa (fee: 29 BD). Bank statement showing minimum regular monthly income of 300–500 BD required. Application submitted online via evisa.gov.bh; processing 3–5 working days.

Traveller must hold a current valid visit visa for a GCC country (Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, or UAE). Entitles holder to apply for a 3-month multiple-entry visit eVisa via evisa.gov.bh. The official NPRA eligibility form (evisa.gov.bh/VISA/visaInput) asks 'Do you have a current valid Residence Permit or Visit Visa for a GCC country?' as a qualifier.

Holders of Indian diplomatic or official/special passports are exempt from visa for stays up to 90 days, per bilateral visa exemption agreement between Bahrain and India signed 15 July 2018, entered into force 19 December 2018.

agreements: GCC (Gulf Cooperation Council) - free movement and right of residence for GCC nationals among member states (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE)

visa_types.name: eVisa – 2-Week Single Entry Visit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Short-term tourism or personal visit to Bahrain. Allows a single entry stay of up to two weeks.
visa_types.notes: Available to nationals of 211 countries listed on evisa.gov.bh. Can also be issued on arrival at the airport for eligible nationalities. Employment strictly prohibited on this visa. Visa can be renewed through NPRA.

visa_types.name: eVisa – 1-Month Multiple Entry Visit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows multiple entries into Bahrain for tourism or personal visits over a one-month validity period.
visa_types.notes: Available via the official Bahrain e-visa portal. Applicants must have a valid reason for multiple visits and meet eligibility criteria.

visa_types.name: eVisa – 3-Month Multiple Entry Visit Visa (GCC Resident)
visa_types.purpose: Allows GCC-resident foreign nationals to visit Bahrain multiple times over a 3-month period.
visa_types.notes: Requires a valid GCC residence permit with at least 3 months remaining validity. Not available to all nationalities - specifically designed for non-GCC-citizen residents of GCC countries.

visa_types.name: Visa on Arrival
visa_types.purpose: Grants entry to eligible foreign nationals at Bahrain International Airport without prior visa application.
visa_types.notes: Available to nationals of 204 countries as listed on the official evisa.gov.bh on-arrival page. Default duration is 2 weeks per UK government travel advice; up to 6-month visit visa also available. Approval is at immigration officer's discretion. Employment strictly prohibited.

visa_types.name: 10-Year Multiple Entry Tourist Visa (Embassy)
visa_types.purpose: Long-validity multiple entry visa for tourism purposes, available through Bahrain embassies abroad.
visa_types.notes: Referenced in US State Department travel advisory. Available through the Bahrain embassy. Individual entry stay duration not specified in available sources.

visa_types.name: Business eVisa
visa_types.purpose: Permits foreign nationals to visit Bahrain for business meetings, conferences, or commercial activities without taking up employment.
visa_types.notes: Available through the official e-visa portal. Applicants must demonstrate a business purpose. Cannot be used to take up employment.

visa_types.name: Work Visa / Employment Visa
visa_types.purpose: Authorises foreign nationals to reside and work in Bahrain under a specific employer's sponsorship.
visa_types.notes: Issued by the Labour Market Regulatory Authority (LMRA). Requires employer sponsorship. Applicants must enter on a visit visa first in many cases, then convert to a residence permit and work permit. Golden Residency holders must still obtain a separate work permit from LMRA to legally work.

visa_types.name: Journalism Visa
visa_types.purpose: Required for accredited journalists visiting Bahrain to cover news or media events.
visa_types.notes: Advance application is mandatory. Referenced in US State Department travel advisory for Bahrain. Must be obtained before travel.

visa_types.name: Golden Residency – Property Owner Track
visa_types.purpose: Long-term residency for foreign nationals who invest in Bahraini real estate meeting the minimum property value threshold.
visa_types.notes: Requires personal share value of BHD 130,000 (approx USD 345,000) or more at time of purchase. Multiple properties can be combined to meet threshold. Application fee BHD 5 (approx USD 13); issuance fee BHD 300 (approx USD 795). Same fees apply per dependent. No minimum stay requirement to maintain residency. Dependents (spouse, parents, children of any age) can be sponsored. Work permit from LMRA required separately to work. Processing 5 working days.

visa_types.name: Golden Residency – Professional/Employee Track
visa_types.purpose: Long-term residency for skilled foreign professionals with a sustained employment history in Bahrain.
visa_types.notes: Requires 5+ years of continuous employment in Bahrain with average monthly basic salary over BHD 2,000 (approx USD 5,300). Must maintain continuous Social Insurance Organization (SIO) coverage. Bonuses and allowances excluded from salary calculation. Application fee BHD 5; issuance fee BHD 300. Processing 5 working days.

visa_types.name: Golden Residency – Retiree Track (Resident)
visa_types.purpose: Long-term residency for foreign nationals who have retired after a long career in Bahrain.
visa_types.notes: For retirees resident in Bahrain: requires 15+ years of work in Bahrain with average pension of BHD 2,000/month or more. Application fee BHD 5; issuance fee BHD 300. Processing 5 working days. No minimum stay requirement to maintain status.

visa_types.name: Golden Residency – Retiree Track (Non-Resident)
visa_types.purpose: Long-term residency for foreign retirees based outside Bahrain who receive a qualifying pension.
visa_types.notes: For non-resident retirees: requires average pension of BHD 4,000/month or more. Application fee BHD 5; issuance fee BHD 300. Processing 5 working days.

visa_types.name: Golden Residency – Talented Individual Track
visa_types.purpose: Long-term residency for entrepreneurs, scientists, researchers, artists, athletes, and other exceptional individuals recognised for their achievements.
visa_types.notes: Subject to committee review and approval. Eligible categories include entrepreneurs with demonstrated business success, researchers and scientists, artists and creatives with recognised work, technology/business innovators, athletes with notable achievements, and other exceptional talent. Application fee BHD 5; issuance fee BHD 300. Processing up to 10 working days.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic / Official Passport Visa Exemption
visa_types.purpose: Allows holders of diplomatic or official passports from countries with bilateral agreements to enter Bahrain without a visa.
visa_types.notes: Bahrain has bilateral visa exemption agreements with select countries for diplomatic and official passport holders. Confirmed for India (signed 15 July 2018, in force 19 December 2018). GCC nationals enter using national ID cards regardless of passport type. Other bilateral agreements exist but Bahrain's MOFA website (mofa.gov.bh) was inaccessible for comprehensive list.

validation_notes: Re-verified against live official sources on 2026-06-13 using direct scrape of evisa.gov.bh via Firecrawl stealth proxy.
VoA list confirmed at 204 countries (live scrape, HTTP 200) - matches existing record count.
E-visa list confirmed at 211 countries (live scrape, HTTP 200) - matches existing record count.
Golden Residency details re-confirmed: property threshold BHD 130,000, professional track BHD 2,000/month avg basic salary, fees BHD 5 application + BHD 300 issuance - all match existing record.
Myanmar entry name corrected to 'Myanmar (formerly Burma)' to match official site label on both VoA and e-visa pages.
Source note corrected: prior entry said '212 nationalities' for the 2-week single-entry eVisa service page; live e-visa list has 211 entries, not 212.

VoA 'Conditions apply' note clarified by FAQ: conditions refer to border officer discretion at entry, not credential pre-requirement; VoA eligibility is by nationality only per official eligibility checker.
No new countries added or removed vs prior record on either list as of 2026-06-13.
The source URL 'https://www.bahrain.bh/wps/portal/.../ContentDetailsPage/!...' (GCC nationals visa-free) remains verified for GCC free movement.
goldenresidency.gov.bh/faqs/ re-confirmed fee structure and dependents policy (unchanged).

2026-06-27 re-verification: goldenresidency.gov.bh/eligibility-criteria/ live fetch confirmed - property BHD 130,000, professional BHD 2,000+/month (5y min, continuous SIO), resident retiree BHD 2,000+/month (15y min in Bahrain), non-resident retiree BHD 4,000+/month - all unchanged.

2026-06-27 re-verification: bahrain.bh Visas page (fetched live) explicitly names ALL five GCC nations - Kuwait, Oman, United Arab Emirates, Saudi Arabia, AND Qatar - as visa-exempt. Existing record's GCC visa-free grouping (including Qatar) is confirmed correct.

2026-06-27 re-verification: evisa.gov.bh/list-of-onarrival-visa-country.html and evisa.gov.bh/list-of-online-visa-country.html both return HTTP 403 (Firecrawl offline this run). Existing VoA (204) and e-visa (211) country lists preserved from 2026-06-13 scrape; data unchanged.

2026-06-27 CRITICAL DISCREPANCY NOTED: Official bahrain.bh Visa on Arrival page (fetched live, URL: https://www.bahrain.bh/wps/portal/en/BNP/HomeNationalPortal/ContentDetailsPage/!ut/p/z1/pVPL...) states 'Visitors from 69 countries can receive visas upon arrival at Bahrain International Airport.' This contradicts the 204-country count in the visa_on_arrival list (sourced from evisa.gov.bh/list-of-onarrival-visa-country.html, scraped 2026-06-13). The evisa.gov.bh VoA page cannot be re-scraped this run (HTTP 403). The 204-country VoA list is preserved unchanged per safety-first rule (do not delete data you cannot re-confirm). The '69 countries' figure may reflect a different classification or page staleness on bahrain.bh. Priority action: re-scrape evisa.gov.bh/list-of-onarrival-visa-country.html when Firecrawl is available to resolve this discrepancy.

2026-06-29 re-verification: evisa.gov.bh pages return HTTP 403 again (Firecrawl offline). goldenresidency.gov.bh/eligibility-criteria/ fetched live - all Golden Residency thresholds unchanged: property BHD 130,000, professional BHD 2,000+/month 5y+, resident retiree BHD 2,000+/month 15y+, non-resident retiree BHD 4,000+/month.

2026-06-29 re-verification: bahrain.bh Visas page (fetched live, URL ending fY27DoJA...) confirms VoA = 69 countries, eVisa = 209 countries. The GCC exempt list on this page names ONLY Kuwait, Oman, Saudi Arabia, and the United Arab Emirates - Qatar is NOT listed. This conflicts with the 2026-06-27 validation note. Because the 2026-06-27 bahrain.bh fetch explicitly named Qatar, and Qatar is a full GCC member with bilateral movement rights established by treaty, the Qatar visa-free entry record is PRESERVED but flagged as requiring future re-confirmation. Per safety rule: cannot disprove without an official statement that Qatar nationals DO need a visa.

2026-06-29 CONFIRMED VoA DISCREPANCY: Two separate bahrain.bh official pages fetched live on this run both state '69 countries' for VoA. The eVisa count on one page is 209 (not 211 as in our e_visa list). The visa_on_arrival list (204 entries) and e_visa list (211 entries) in this record are preserved from the 2026-06-13 evisa.gov.bh direct scrape, but their accuracy is NOW DOUBLE-FLAGGED as suspect: the official bahrain.bh government summary pages give 69 VoA / 209 e-visa, both lower than the evisa.gov.bh list counts. The most likely explanation: the evisa.gov.bh 'on-arrival' page lists all nationalities that can obtain any type of Bahrain visa electronically (not just at-airport VoA), while the actual at-airport VoA is restricted to 69 nationalities.
gaps: CRITICAL (double-confirmed 2026-06-29): VoA country count discrepancy - official bahrain.bh Visa on Arrival page says '69 countries' eligible for VoA; the evisa.gov.bh/list-of-onarrival-visa-country.html page (last scraped 2026-06-13) contained 204 entries. These two official Bahrain government sources are in direct conflict. The 204-entry list is preserved but its accuracy as a pure VoA list is unresolved. Must re-scrape evisa.gov.bh when available (currently returns HTTP 403). The evisa.gov.bh 'on-arrival' page may conflate VoA eligibility with general e-visa eligibility under a shared NPRA portal system.

CRITICAL: Qatar GCC visa-free status - the 2026-06-29 live fetch of bahrain.bh Visas page lists ONLY Kuwait, Oman, Saudi Arabia, UAE as GCC-exempt (Qatar not named). The 2026-06-27 run found a different bahrain.bh page that included Qatar. Qatar is a GCC member by treaty. Qatar entry kept as visa-free per preservation rule and GCC treaty basis. Must re-confirm via a direct Qatar-specific official Bahraini source.

eVisa count discrepancy: bahrain.bh says 209 countries; record has 211 from 2026-06-13 evisa.gov.bh scrape. 2-country difference. Cannot identify which nationalities differ without re-scraping evisa.gov.bh.

Visa-on-arrival per-country stay duration: The official on-arrival page (evisa.gov.bh/list-of-onarrival-visa-country.html) lists nationalities all with 'Conditions apply' but does not display stay duration as a table column. Per-country stay details require clicking individual country links. Max stay duration not confirmed from the list page itself.

E-visa max stay duration: The evisa.gov.bh eligibility list does not specify per-nationality max stay days; individual visa types (1-week, 2-week, 1-month, 3-month, etc.) are available but nationality-to-duration mapping requires per-country click-through not available as a table.
Dual citizenship policy for naturalized citizens: NPRA naturalization page does not address dual citizenship allowance.
No digital nomad or dedicated remote-work visa found on official Bahrain government sources as of re-verification date (June 2026).
Investor residency (2/5/10-year tracks at BHD 100,000 threshold) details minimal - from bahrain.bh search result summary only, full official page details not scraped.

Conditional access - US/UK/Schengen visa holders: The evisa.gov.bh eligibility form only asks about GCC credentials, not US/UK/Schengen visa holders. No official Bahrain government page confirmed a separate conditional entry pathway based solely on holding a US, UK, or Schengen visa; only GCC residence permit/visa is explicitly listed as a conditional qualifier. Aggregator sources (travel blogs) mention US B1/B2 holders can get VoA, but this appears to be a general VoA eligibility (US nationals are on the VoA list), not a credential-based conditional rule. No OCI-cardholder-specific Bahrain entry rule found on any official source. Diplomatic/service passport bilateral agreements beyond India could not be confirmed from Bahrain-side official sources (mofa.gov.bh was inaccessible); India mea.gov.in confirms Bahrain-India agreement (diplomatic/official passports).

https://goldenresidency.gov.bh/eligibility-criteria/ (re-verified live 2026-06-27 and 2026-06-29 - all Golden Residency thresholds confirmed unchanged: property BHD 130,000, professional BHD 2,000+/month avg basic salary 5y+, resident retiree BHD 2,000+/month 15y+, non-resident retiree BHD 4,000+/month)
